message:
🐛 Fix cards not showing cached data by always mounting children (#493)

* 🐛 Fix offline banner and card flickering during agent connection

- Layout.tsx: Only show offline banner when agent is confirmed
  'disconnected', not during 'connecting' state
- CardWrapper.tsx: Track if agent was ever connected - only force
  skeleton after losing a previously established connection

This prevents UI flickering during the connecting → connected
transition on page load.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix UI flickering and cache clearing on navigation

- Fix stat blocks flickering during agent connection by checking
  for 'disconnected' status only (not 'connecting')
- Fix hooks clearing cached data on initial mount by tracking
  previous parameter values with useRef
- Affected hooks: useServices, usePodIssues, useDeployments,
  useNodes, useNamespaces
- Fixed files: CardWrapper, StatsOverview, Security, Clusters,
  Workloads, MissionSuggestions

The root cause was useEffect clearing state on every component
mount, even when the dependency values hadn't actually changed.
Now state only resets when cluster/namespace parameters change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🌱 Add initial skeleton period in CardWrapper for fresh loads

- Show skeleton for 300ms on mount while waiting for cards to report state
- Cards that report state via useReportCardDataState immediately control skeleton
- Cards that don't report get 300ms skeleton then show their content
- Prevents blank cards on initial page load

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix cards not showing cached data by always mounting children

The skeleton rendering logic had a deadlock: CardWrapper waited for
children to report hasData via useCardLoadingState, but children
weren't rendered at all when shouldShowSkeleton was true.

Changes:
- CardWrapper: Always render children (hidden with CSS when skeleton
  shows) so they can mount and report their data state
- CardDataContext: Use useLayoutEffect instead of useEffect so state
  reports happen synchronously before paint

This fixes the stale-while-revalidate pattern where cached data should
display immediately while a background refresh happens.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -154,9 +154,9 @@ export function StatsOverview({
   const { status: agentStatus } = useLocalAgent()
   const { isDemoMode } = useDemoMode()
 
-  // When demo mode is OFF and agent is not connected, force skeleton display
-  // Include 'connecting' state because it may take time before we know agent is truly offline
-  const isAgentOffline = agentStatus !== 'connected'
+  // When demo mode is OFF and agent is confirmed disconnected, force skeleton display
+  // Don't force skeleton during 'connecting' - show cached data to prevent flicker
+  const isAgentOffline = agentStatus === 'disconnected'
   const forceLoadingForOffline = !isDemoMode && !isDemoData && isAgentOffline
   const effectiveIsLoading = isLoading || forceLoadingForOffline
   const effectiveHasData = forceLoadingForOffline ? false : hasData

@@ -46,7 +46,7 @@ export function Workloads() {
   const isLoading = podIssuesLoading || deploymentIssuesLoading || deploymentsLoading || clustersLoading
   const isRefreshing = podIssuesRefreshing || deploymentIssuesRefreshing || deploymentsRefreshing
   // Show skeletons when loading with no data OR when agent is offline and demo mode is OFF
-  const isAgentOffline = agentStatus !== 'connected'
+  const isAgentOffline = agentStatus === 'disconnected'
   const forceSkeletonForOffline = !isDemoMode && isAgentOffline
   const showSkeletons = ((allDeployments.length === 0 && podIssues.length === 0 && deploymentIssues.length === 0) && isLoading) || forceSkeletonForOffline

@@ -1,4 +1,4 @@
-import { useState, useEffect, useCallback } from 'react'
+import { useState, useEffect, useCallback, useRef } from 'react'
 import { api } from '../../lib/api'
 import { reportAgentDataSuccess, isAgentUnavailable } from '../useLocalAgent'
 import { getDemoMode } from '../useDemoMode'
@@ -69,11 +69,25 @@ export function useServices(cluster?: string, namespace?: string) {
   const [consecutiveFailures, setConsecutiveFailures] = useState(0)
   const [lastRefresh, setLastRefresh] = useState<Date | null>(cached?.timestamp || null)
 
-  // Reset state when cluster changes
+  // Track previous cluster/namespace to detect actual changes (not just initial mount)
+  const prevClusterRef = useRef<string | undefined>(cluster)
+  const prevNamespaceRef = useRef<string | undefined>(namespace)
+
+  // Reset state only when cluster/namespace actually CHANGES (not on initial mount)
   useEffect(() => {
-    setServices([])
-    setIsLoading(true)
-    setError(null)
+    const clusterChanged = prevClusterRef.current !== cluster
+    const namespaceChanged = prevNamespaceRef.current !== namespace
+
+    if (clusterChanged || namespaceChanged) {
+      // Only reset if values actually changed
+      setServices([])
+      setIsLoading(true)
+      setError(null)
+
+      // Update refs to new values
+      prevClusterRef.current = cluster
+      prevNamespaceRef.current = namespace
+    }
   }, [cluster, namespace])
 
   const refetch = useCallback(async (silent = false) => {

   const [lastRefresh, setLastRefresh] = useState<Date | null>(cached?.timestamp || null)
 
-  // Reset state when cluster changes
+  // Track previous values to detect actual changes (not just initial mount)
+  const prevClusterRef = useRef<string | undefined>(cluster)

   useEffect(() => {

-    setError(null)
